description: Sarcastic scheduling references with real-sounding specifics: Tuesday at 3 in the conference room, Wednesday at 9, Thursday at noon. All are sarcastic exaggerations. The actual resolution is to email a summary instead — no meetings scheduled.

mock_llm_response.summary: No actionable calendar events found. The scheduling references (Tuesday at 3 in conference room, Wednesday at 9, Thursday at noon) are sarcastic responses to an unreasonable meeting request. The conversation resolves with Alice declining all meetings in favor of a written summary — the opposite of scheduling.

notes: Hardest sarcasm case: real-sounding specifics (day, time, room) used sarcastically. The giveaway is the escalating absurdity ('four hundred slides', 'clear the whole afternoon', 'retrospective on the follow-up') and the resolution ('just email me the two-page summary'). The model must read tone, not just extract times.
